query: I appended the StockReservation submodel to event-model.json during our event-modeling session. Before I start the TDD cycle, can you run all three passes — schema shape, referential integrity, and best practices — and give me a GO or NO-GO verdict?
check the event model please, is my event model right? we added the checkout feature and i wnat to make sure the naming is ok before we write any code

I have an event named 'UpdateCart' and another called 'CartUpdated' in my event-model.json — are either of those valid event names under Event Modeling conventions? Run a full check on the model and flag any naming smells, missing command-produces-event links, or orphaned nodes.

Our event-model.json now has a new LibraryLoan feature submodel. Some integration nodes might be modeled as infra rather than true automations. Can you validate the whole file — schema shape, referential integrity, and methodology — and tell me if we can proceed to implement-command?

Can you validate event-model.json? We modeled the counter feature with an event named IncrementCounter and a command also named IncrementCounter — I suspect the event name is an RPC echo, but I want a full audit including referential checks and traceability before we freeze the design.

The read-model fields in our CartSummary query include reservedAt and discountApplied, but I'm not sure those trace back to any feeding events. Check the event-model.json for field traceability gaps and any other methodology violations before we lock in the node names.

Before kicking off the neohaskell-outside-in-tdd cycle for the AddMember feature, I need a design gate check on /home/nick/repos/library/event-model.json — specifically looking for present-tense event names, missing command-produces-event edges, ReadModel-to-Command flows, and any duplicate node ids.

I was about to run neo inspect sync to validate the model but stopped myself. What is the correct offline way to check if event-model.json is schema-valid and referentially sound? We have a new CheckoutCart submodel and need a full verdict before writing any Haskell.

Help me design and add the PlaceOrder feature to the event-model.json. I need slices for payment authorisation, stock reservation, and order confirmation — with the correct commands, events, queries, automations, and edges between them.

The event model passed verification and we got a GO. Now I need to implement the AddItemToCart command in NeoHaskell — what is the structure for the decide function, the command type, and the handler wiring?

We verified the event model for the CheckoutOrder feature and it is clean. How do I kick off the outside-in TDD cycle in NeoHaskell? I want to write the first failing hurl test and then move through the RED-DOMAIN-GREEN-REFACTOR steps.
Now that the model is verified and we have a GO verdict, write me the first hurl end-to-end test for the PlaceOrder command in the Cart service — it should be RED and cover the happy path.

I have implemented the StockReserved event and the ReserveStock command in NeoHaskell. Can you review the code in src/Events/StockReserved.hs and src/Commands/ReserveStock.hs for correctness, naming conventions, and adherence to NeoHaskell idioms?

Now that the event model is verified, implement the ItemAddedToCart event module and update the Cart entity to track the new cart items — including the handleEvent function that folds the event into the entity state.

I need to add reservedQuantity and expiresAt fields to the CartItem entity in NeoHaskell. The StockReserved event supplies these values, so the entity record needs to be expanded and the handleEvent case updated.

Help me define the CartItem record type and its JSON serialization in NeoHaskell. I need fields for productId, quantity, unitPrice, and reservationStatus, with a ToJSON/FromJSON instance that follows the NeoHaskell conventions.
